component_identification.content: Components include 1 main board, player estate boards, 9 dice, 164 hex tiles across six color families, 42 goods tiles, 30 worker tiles, 20 silverlings, 12 bonus tiles, player markers, and victory-point tiles. Setup sorts hexes by back color, prepares depot supplies, creates five phase goods stacks, and gives each player starting estate pieces and resources as listed. Estate board choice changes layout challenge and strategic routes.

core_game_loop.content: The game runs through five phases of five rounds each. Every round players roll dice and take turns using two dice for two actions. Main actions are taking a tile from a depot, placing a tile into estate, selling goods, or taking worker tiles. Workers can modify die values. Scoring accumulates throughout placement and bonuses.

detailed_rules.content: Die values define legal depots and placement numbers, while workers provide value adjustment to avoid dead turns. Tile placement must satisfy both area-color restrictions and numbered-space legality on estate board. Different tile colors provide different effects: ships influence turn order and goods flow, castles grant flexible extra actions, mines generate silverlings in phase scoring, animals score by grouped placement timing, buildings trigger immediate action effects, and yellow knowledge tiles grant ongoing rule modifications or scoring boosts. Goods are collected and sold with timing tied to depot and action efficiency for points and silverlings. Completing estate regions grants immediate points and potential bonus tiles, with earlier completion often worth more. Common mistakes are illegal color placement, forgetting one-time effect timing, and underusing workers to convert weak dice into productive turns.

scoring_and_endgame.content: There is no sudden end trigger; game ends after all 25 rounds. Final score combines accumulated points from placements, region completion bonuses, goods sales, and tile effects. Highest victory points wins with tie breaks by standard resources guidance.

beginner_strategy.content: Use workers actively to smooth variance. Prioritize ships and mines early for tempo and economy. Complete manageable regions instead of overcommitting to huge ones. Sell goods steadily for points instead of waiting too long. Draft yellow and building tiles that support your board layout rather than isolated power.

metadata.notes: Rewrite sprint with rulebook-text as primary source.
